payload.source_text: The pocket money tracker system allows children to claim pocket money tasks as they are completed throughout the week.
Parents can detail tasks that should be completed and the monetary amount earned for that task.
As a task is completed it is linked to the child that completed it.
The children should be able to see the available tasks and the tasks they have completed that week.
At the end of each week the children can see the total amount they have earned that week.
The Parents can also see a report of how much each child has earned and the tasks they completed so that they can provide the pocket money earned to each child.
At the start of the next week all tasks become available again to be claimed.
Historical task data shall be available.
The resulting system shall be able to operate on iOS 16, Android 9 and Windows 11 or later.

payload.description: Task claiming and completion tracking process
payload.justification: Enabling children to claim and track completion represents a core strategic process for the enterprise.

payload.description: Task definition and reward assignment process
payload.justification: Parental definition of tasks and association of rewards constitutes a distinct strategic process enabling the enterprise.

payload.description: Actionable payment reporting process
payload.justification: Obtaining actionable reports to inform payment decisions is a clear strategic process supporting parental business objectives.

payload.description: Task visibility provision to children
payload.justification: Providing visibility of available and completed tasks represents a strategic function enabling transparency and engagement.

payload.description: Earnings total presentation to children
payload.justification: Enabling children to view weekly earnings totals is a motivational function strategically important to the enterprise.

payload.description: Historical task record retention
payload.justification: Maintaining historical records for continuity or accountability is a strategic function supporting enterprise governance.

payload.description: Pocket money task as a strategic work unit
payload.justification: Multiple signals reference tasks as the core entity enabling the enterprise purpose: claiming and tracking completion (SG915), defining and associating rewards (SG916), and providing visibility (SG917).

payload.description: Task completion record as a strategic accountability artifact
payload.justification: Completion tracking (SG915), visibility of completed tasks (SG917), and historical record maintenance (SG920) collectively point to completion records as a distinct strategic entity.

payload.description: Monetary reward as a strategic value exchange mechanism
payload.justification: Associating monetary rewards with tasks (SG916) and viewing earnings totals (SG918) establish monetary value as a strategic entity central to the enterprise model.

payload.description: Weekly earnings total as a strategic performance metric
payload.justification: Weekly totals represent a calculated attribute enabling motivational visibility, distinct from individual task rewards.

payload.description: Association between task and monetary reward
payload.justification: SG916 explicitly describes defining tasks and associating monetary rewards with completion, establishing a strategic relationship.

payload.description: Weekly earnings aggregation cycle
payload.justification: Weekly earnings totals imply a strategic weekly cycle for aggregating and presenting financial performance.

payload.description: Child as task claimant and tracker
payload.justification: Children are identified as the strategic actor who claims tasks (SG915), views task visibility (SG917), and monitors earnings (SG918).

payload.description: Parent as task definer and payment decision maker
payload.justification: Parents are the strategic actor defining tasks and rewards (SG916) and using reports to inform payment decisions (SG919).

payload.description: Enable children to claim and complete pocket money tasks
payload.justification: SG915 directly expresses the core enterprise purpose as a strategic goal.

payload.description: Motivate children through earnings visibility
payload.justification: The motivational goal of enabling earnings visibility is explicitly identified as a strategic objective.

payload.description: Enable parental definition and reward association for tasks
payload.justification: Parental motivation to define and reward tasks represents a strategic goal supporting enterprise operations.

payload.description: Inform parental payment decisions through actionable reporting
payload.justification: The business objective of obtaining actionable reports is a clear strategic goal.

payload.description: Provide task visibility to children
payload.justification: The purpose of providing visibility to available and completed tasks is articulated as a strategic goal.

payload.description: Maintain historical accountability through task record retention
payload.justification: The strategic requirement for continuity or accountability through historical records represents a guiding principle for the enterprise.

payload.description: Define task with monetary value
payload.justification: Parent capability to define tasks represents a core business function.

payload.description: View available tasks and completion records
payload.justification: Child capability to view tasks and their completion status represents a business function.

payload.description: View aggregated earnings
payload.justification: Child capability to access earnings data represents a business function.

payload.description: Access child-task-earnings reports for payment fulfillment
payload.justification: Parent capability to access reports for payment processing represents a core business function.

payload.description: Completed tasks must be linked to child entities
payload.justification: Normative signal explicitly defines a business rule governing task-child relationships.

payload.description: Task availability resets at weekly interval boundaries
payload.justification: Normative signal defines a business rule governing temporal behavior of task availability.

payload.description: Historical task completion entities must persist temporally
payload.justification: Normative signal establishes a business requirement for data retention as a rule.

payload.description: Child entity participating in task completion and earnings tracking
payload.justification: Child is a core business entity that completes tasks and accumulates earnings, explicitly mentioned in the business concept and child actor capabilities.

payload.description: Task entity with associated monetary value
payload.justification: Task is a fundamental business entity that can be defined by parents, completed by children, and has monetary attributes.

payload.description: Task completion record linking child to completed task
payload.justification: Completion records are distinct entities that establish the relationship between children and tasks, with temporal persistence requirements.

payload.description: Earnings data aggregated for child over weekly period
payload.justification: Pocket money claims are business entities derived from task completions within weekly cycles. | Aggregated earnings represent a distinct business entity viewable by children at temporal boundaries.

payload.description: Report entity showing child-task-earnings relationships
payload.justification: Reports are business entities accessed by parents to fulfill payment obligations.

payload.description: Monetary value attribute of task entity
payload.justification: Monetary value is explicitly identified as an attribute associated with task entities.

payload.description: Relationship between child entity and completed task entity
payload.justification: Business rule explicitly defines the linking relationship between completed tasks and child entities.

payload.description: Weekly pocket money cycle
payload.justification: Weekly cycle is a fundamental temporal pattern governing task availability, completion tracking, and earnings aggregation.

payload.description: Week end event triggering earnings aggregation
payload.justification: Week end represents a significant business event when earnings become visible to children.

payload.description: Weekly interval boundary triggering task availability reset
payload.justification: Weekly boundary acts as a trigger condition that initiates the reset of task availability.

payload.description: Parent role defining tasks and accessing reports
payload.justification: Parent is identified as a business role with specific capabilities for task definition and payment fulfillment.

payload.description: Child role completing tasks and viewing earnings
payload.justification: Child is identified as a business role with specific capabilities for task completion and earnings visibility.

payload.description: Enable children to earn pocket money through task completion
payload.justification: Core business concept reveals the fundamental business goal of linking tasks to earnings.

payload.description: Task completion visibility limited to current week
payload.justification: Temporal limitation on child's view of completion records represents a business constraint.

payload.description: Historical task completion data must be preserved
payload.justification: Requirement for temporal persistence reflects a business principle governing data management.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL CellContentItem elements present in the ledger.

payload.name: Child Participation and Entitlement
payload.description: Enterprise commitment to enabling children's engagement with value-generating activities and their entitlement to visibility of earned rewards and available opportunities.

payload.name: Parental Governance and Stewardship
payload.description: Enterprise accountability for defining scope of work, establishing value exchange terms, and making informed disbursement decisions based on performance evidence.

payload.name: Work-Reward Exchange Framework
payload.description: Enterprise obligation to maintain a transparent framework linking completed work units to monetary value, including performance metrics and exchange mechanisms.

payload.name: Performance Accountability and Transparency
payload.description: Enterprise responsibility for capturing, preserving, and presenting evidence of work completion to support both participant motivation and authoritative decision-making.

payload.name: Task Stewardship
payload.description: Business responsibility for establishing and governing the task catalog, including the definition of tasks with their associated monetary values and the rules governing task availability across temporal boundaries.

payload.name: Task Completion Record
payload.description: Business responsibility for maintaining the authoritative record of task completion events, linking participants to their completed obligations and preserving this record across time for accountability and evidence purposes.

payload.name: Earnings Accountability
payload.description: Business responsibility for establishing participant entitlement to earnings based on completed tasks, governed by temporal cycles, including aggregation of earnings over defined periods and reporting for settlement fulfillment.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Domain elements present in the ledger.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable children to claim and complete value-generating activities.
payload.rationale: Row 1 enterprise commitment derived from the Why-column goal and Who-column actor role; expresses the foundational entitlement children have to participate in the enterprise's reward-based activity model.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall provide children visibility of available value-generating activities.
payload.rationale: Consolidates the How-column function (task visibility provision) and corresponding Why-column goal into a single enterprise commitment; subject is enterprise, action is provide, object is visibility of activities.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall provide children visibility of their aggregate earned rewards.
payload.rationale: Consolidates the How-column function (earnings total presentation) and corresponding Why-column goal (motivate through earnings visibility) into a single enterprise commitment; distinct from task visibility, this addresses reward transparency.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall establish processes for defining tasks and assigning rewards.
payload.rationale: Expresses the enterprise's commitment to maintaining a structured approach for task definition and reward association, directly supporting parental governance responsibilities.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall provide actionable reporting to inform payment decisions.
payload.rationale: Captures the enterprise obligation to enable informed disbursement decisions through reporting capabilities, central to the stewardship function.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall be accountable for task definition and payment decision-making authority.
payload.rationale: Establishes the governance accountability boundary — the enterprise retains authority for defining scope of work and making disbursement decisions based on performance evidence.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall establish work units as strategic instruments for value creation.
payload.rationale: Entity CCI describes pocket money task as strategic work unit; at Row 1 this surfaces as an enterprise structural commitment to recognise work units as fundamental instruments.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall recognise monetary rewards as strategic value exchange mechanisms.
payload.rationale: Entity CCI identifies monetary reward as a strategic exchange mechanism; this requires enterprise-level recognition of the instrument's structural role.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall maintain a performance metric representing aggregated earnings over a defined period.
payload.rationale: Attribute CCI defines weekly earnings total as strategic performance metric; at enterprise scope this is a commitment to maintain the metric as a structural element of accountability.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall establish an association between work units and monetary rewards.
payload.rationale: Relationship CCI explicitly describes the association between task and reward; this surfaces as an enterprise structural commitment to link the two entities.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall operate a recurring cycle for aggregating earnings.
payload.rationale: When-column CCI describes a weekly aggregation cycle; at enterprise scope this is an obligation to perform the recurring aggregation activity.
payload.fit_criteria: Earnings aggregation cycle occurs at regular, defined intervals.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall maintain task completion records as authoritative evidence of performance accountability.
payload.rationale: Enterprise-level commitment to preserve evidence of work completion for strategic accountability; combines the structural definition of task completion records as accountability artifacts with the governance principle requiring their retention.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall establish processes for task claiming and completion tracking.
payload.rationale: Enterprise commitment to enable the operational capability for participants to claim tasks and track completion status; process-level capability establishing accountability chain.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall preserve historical task records to support long-term accountability and transparency.
payload.rationale: Enterprise obligation to maintain access to historical performance data; combines the retention function with the accountability principle it serves.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain a catalog of tasks, each with an associated monetary value.
payload.rationale: Business-subject structural obligation describing the task entity and its required monetary value attribute as a maintained business artifact.

payload.statement: The business shall enforce task availability reset at weekly interval boundaries.
payload.rationale: Business rule constraining task availability behavior according to temporal boundaries. The weekly reset is a governance constraint on business behavior, not a workflow step.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain a task completion record linking each child to their completed task.
payload.rationale: Core business responsibility for maintaining the authoritative record of task completion events and the structural relationship between child entities and completed task entities.

payload.statement: The business shall link each completed task to the child entity that completed it.
payload.rationale: Business rule governing the mandatory association between completed tasks and child entities, ensuring accountability.

payload.statement: The business shall preserve historical task completion records across time.
payload.rationale: Business governance rule requiring temporal persistence of completion data for accountability and evidence purposes.

payload.statement: A child shall be recognised as a participant entitled to claim earnings from completed tasks.
payload.rationale: Establishes the child entity as a participant in the earnings accountability domain with entitlement to earnings based on task completion.

payload.statement: The system shall make aggregated earnings visible to the entitled child.
payload.rationale: System-subject affordance complementing the child's view capability — what the system provides at the boundary.

payload.statement: The system shall make child-task-earnings reports available to parents.
payload.rationale: System-subject affordance providing report access capability at the boundary.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain aggregated earnings data for each child over the weekly period.
payload.rationale: Business responsibility to maintain artefact (earnings data) governed by temporal cycle.

payload.statement: Aggregated earnings data shall consist of child identifier, weekly period, and earnings amount.
payload.rationale: Structural composition of the earnings aggregation entity required to support accountability.

payload.statement: A child-task-earnings report shall present the relationship between child, completed tasks, and associated earnings.
payload.rationale: Structural definition of report entity showing accountability relationships.

payload.statement: The business shall aggregate child earnings at the end of each week.
payload.rationale: Business responsibility triggered by week-end event to perform earnings aggregation.

payload.statement: The business shall enforce the weekly pocket money cycle for earnings accountability.
payload.rationale: Temporal governance constraint establishing the weekly cycle as the earnings accountability period.

payload.statement: The business shall limit task completion visibility to the current week.
payload.rationale: Why-column constraint governing visibility scope to align with weekly cycle.

payload.statement: The system shall make task completion earnable by children as pocket money.
payload.rationale: CCI-ROW2-C-Why-001 is a Why-column Goal expressing the enabling of earnings through task completion. At Row 2, this translates to a black-box system affordance (WHAT the system provides at the boundary). The system subject is appropriate because the CCI describes a capability the system must offer (making tasks earnable), not an actor action or a behind-the-boundary business responsibility. The verb 'make earnable' names the provided capability without specifying HOW (no API/validation/algorithm), keeping it at Row 2 abstraction. Type is Functional because the CCI is expressing a core system capability (How-column business capability enabled by the system), not a governance rule or constraint.

payload.statement: A parent can define a task with monetary value.
payload.rationale: CCI-ROW2-C-Who-001 identifies the parent role as the external actor defining tasks, and CCI-ROW2-C-How-001 names the capability to define tasks with monetary value. These form a boundary-crossing actor-action statement (ACTOR subject, subject-action-object complete). Both CCIs belong to D005 (Task Stewardship) and together express a single obligation: the parent's capability to create valued tasks. How-column function capability at Row 2.

payload.statement: A parent can access reports.
payload.rationale: CCI-ROW2-C-Who-001 identifies the parent role as accessing reports. This is a boundary-crossing actor capability (ACTOR subject). The parent reaches into the system to access reports, distinct from the task-definition capability. How-column function at Row 2.

payload.statement: The system shall make available tasks and completion records visible.
payload.rationale: CCI-ROW2-C-How-002 expresses the capability to view available tasks and completion records. This is a SYSTEM subject statement (black-box affordance) naming WHAT the system provides at the boundary (making tasks and records visible), not HOW. The system provides visibility of both available tasks and completion records. How-column capability declaration at Row 2, domain D006 (Task Completion Record).

payload.description: Expresses the core purpose of enabling children to claim and track completion of pocket money tasks.
Describes the parental motivation to define tasks and associate monetary rewards with completion.
Articulates the purpose of providing visibility to children regarding available and completed tasks.
Identifies the motivational goal of enabling children to view weekly earnings totals.
Expresses the parental business objective of obtaining actionable reports to inform payment decisions.
Indicates the strategic requirement to maintain historical task records for continuity or accountability purposes.
Document heading introducing the conceptual overview of the system domain.
Core business concept establishing the relationship between children, tasks, and pocket money claims over weekly cycles.
Parent actor capability to define task entities with associated monetary value attributes.
Business rule defining the linking relationship between completed tasks and child entities.
Child actor capability to view both available task entities and their own completion records within the current week.
Child actor capability to view aggregated earnings data at the end of each weekly time period.
Parent actor capability to access reporting entities showing child-task-earnings relationships for payment fulfillment.
Business rule governing the temporal reset of task availability at weekly interval boundaries.
Business requirement for temporal persistence of historical task completion entities.
